content.text:
# DCG.CO — Agent Context File

## Project Root
Z:

## Stack
Next.js 15 App Router, Supabase, Stripe, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TypeScript

## Real Directory Map
- app/ — Next.js routes (App Router)
- components/ — Shared UI components
- components/shop/ — Storefront components
- components/Layouts/ — Layout system
- components/cart/ — Cart components
- lib/ — Shared logic, contexts, utils
- utils/supabase/ — Supabase clients (client.ts, server.ts, middleware.ts)
- hooks/ — Custom React hooks
- types/ — TypeScript types
- style/ — CSS files including layout-tokens.css
- ai/ — Project documentation (ARCHITECTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, DECISIONS.md, TASKS.md)

## Key Files
- ai/CONVENTIONS.md — Coding standards to follow
- ai/ARCHITECTURE.md — Full file tree
- ai/DECISIONS.md — Why things were built the way they were
- ai/TASKS.md — Current task list
- components/Layouts/routeClassifier.ts — Route logic
- utils/supabase/client.ts — Browser Supabase client
- utils/supabase/server.ts — Server Supabase client

## Hard Rules
- NEVER touch: webhooks/stripe, auth/, utils/supabase/middleware.ts
- NEVER delete working code — only improve it
- NEVER run git commands
- NEVER change branding, colors, or business logic
- Keep files under 150 lines
- Server components fetch data, client components handle interaction
- Use existing Tailwind + CSS token system — no inline styles

## Updated Files (2026-03-03)
### components/
- New folders: `orders`, `POS`, `documents`, `profile`, `settings`
- New files: `assignRandomJobs.ts`, `CleanTrack.tsx`, `Export.tsx`, `PunchCardGrid.tsx`, `UniversalExportButton.tsx`

### hooks/
- New hooks: `useAnalyticsConsent.ts`, `useCalendarPermissions.ts`, `useChatDebugActions.ts`, `useDocuments.ts`, `useHallMonitor.ts`, `useMessageManagement.ts`, `useOptimisticHours.ts`, `usePersistentTimesheetLogic.ts`, `useRealtimeInsert.ts`, `useRealtimeNotifications.ts`

### lib/
- New folders: `landing`, `monitors`, `usps`
- New files: `escpos.ts`, `thermalPrinter.ts`, `notifications.ts`, `robustPDFGenerator.ts`, `roleContext.tsx`

content.thinking:
Okay, the user is trying to list the contents of the 'types' directory but keeps running into errors. The initial attempts with 'dir' and PowerShell commands are failing, probably due to incorrect path formatting or syntax issues.

First, I need to figure out why the PowerShell command isn't working. The error messages indicate that '.Name' isn't recognized, which suggests a typo or incorrect usage in the command. The user might have mistakenly used '.Name' instead of the correct property name like 'Name'.

Looking at the command: "Get-ChildItem -Path 'Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\types\' -Force | ForEach-Object { .Name }". The issue is the use of '.Name' inside the script block. In PowerShell, when using the pipeline, the objects passed to the script block are the items from Get-ChildItem. To access their names, it should be $_.Name, not .Name.

So, the corrected command should be: "Get-ChildItem -Path 'Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\types\' -Force | ForEach-Object { $_.Name }". This way, each item's Name property is accessed correctly.

Alternatively, using the 'Select-Object' cmdlet might be more straightforward. For example: "Get-ChildItem -Path 'Z:\WEBSITES\DCG.CO\types\' -Force | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Name". This would directly output the names of the files and directories without needing a ForEach-Object loop.

Another possibility is that the path is incorrect. The user might be using single backslashes instead of double backslashes or not properly escaping the quotes. Ensuring the path is correctly formatted with double backslashes or using single quotes might help.

Also, checking if the 'types' directory actually exists is crucial. If the previous attempts to create it failed, the directory might not be present, leading to the 'Path not found' error. The user should confirm the directory's existence using 'Test-Path' before attempting to list its contents.

In summary, the main issues are likely the incorrect syntax in the PowerShell command and potential path formatting problems. Correcting the command to use $_.Name and verifying the directory's existence should resolve the errors.
